name: Core-Keeper Operator
stronger_vietnamese_name: Toan Tu Tai Cau Hinh Bao Toan Ban Sac
short_definition: The Core-Keeper Operator transforms a system into a new configuration while preserving its structural core, adapting its boundary, cutting obstructive residue, and producing an explanation trace.
conceptual_formula: Omega_CK(S_n, C_n, B_n, R_n, G) -> S_{n+1} + Trace + R_cut
plain_language_formula: current system + core + boundary + residue + goal -> reclosed next system + explanation trace + cut residue
core_thesis: A system can change form without losing identity if its structural core is preserved, its boundary is adapted, and obstructive residue is cut or isolated before reclosure.

origin_mapping.mythic_expression: the being that cannot be rewritten by the world
origin_mapping.operator_expression: a mechanism for preserving identity during transformation

S_n.name: current_state
S_n.description: The current configuration of the system before transformation.

C_n.name: structural_core
C_n.description: The essential contribution, identity, intention, invariant, or value that must be preserved.

B_n.name: current_boundary
B_n.description: The current external form, style, interface, role, context, or presentation layer.

R_n.name: residue
R_n.description: Noise, obsolete framing, overclaiming, outdated constraints, ambiguous metaphors, or fragments that no longer serve the core.

G.name: reconfiguration_goal
G.description: The target context or output form into which the system must be reclosed.

S_n_plus_1.name: reclosed_next_state
S_n_plus_1.description: The transformed configuration after the core is preserved, the boundary is adapted, and residue is handled.

Trace.name: explanation_trace
Trace.description: A structured explanation of what was preserved, changed, cut, reused, and why.

R_cut.name: cut_residue
R_cut.description: The obstructive residue removed from the next configuration or isolated from the core.

metrics.name: core_keeper_metrics
metrics.description: Scores for core fidelity, boundary adaptability, residue clearance, reclosure success, drift resistance, trace clarity, and identity preservation.

pipeline.name: Core Extraction
pipeline.description: Separate the structural core from surface form, presentation layer, and residue.
pipeline.question: What must remain true after transformation?

pipeline.name: Boundary Test
pipeline.description: Determine which boundary elements should be kept, adapted, replaced, or removed.
pipeline.question: Which parts are only form, interface, style, role, or context?

pipeline.name: Residue Cut
pipeline.description: Cut, isolate, reuse, or monitor residue according to whether it obstructs the core.
pipeline.question: What is noise, obsolete structure, or reusable remainder?

pipeline.name: Target Reconfiguration
pipeline.description: Build a candidate next state that fits the target context while carrying the preserved core.
pipeline.question: What new form can carry the same core?

pipeline.name: Core Fidelity Check
pipeline.description: Verify that the core has not been weakened, flattened, or replaced by a convenient surface form.
pipeline.question: Is the new configuration still the same system at the level of structural identity?

pipeline.name: Reclosure
pipeline.description: Close the preserved core, adapted boundary, and reusable residue into a coherent next state.
pipeline.question: Has the system become a usable configuration again?

pipeline.name: Explanation Trace
pipeline.description: Emit a trace that records preservation, alteration, removal, reuse, rationale, and remaining risk.
pipeline.question: Can another reader or system explain the transformation?

core_components.core: The non-negotiable structural identity of the system.
core_components.boundary: The negotiable external form or interface through which the system appears.
core_components.residue: The remainder that may obstruct, distort, or later feed another configuration.
core_components.goal: The target context that requires the system to change form.
core_components.trace: The explanation record of what happened during transformation.

relationship_to_other_operators.Prime-TB Cut: Cuts a system into structural components and exposes core, boundary, residue, and trace.
relationship_to_other_operators.Noir Temporal Cut: Cuts time into possibility, reality lock, replacement, residue, and past trace.
relationship_to_other_operators.Core-Keeper Operator: Preserves identity through transformation after cutting and before reclosure.
relationship_to_other_operators.Prime Excalibur: Forces a configuration to prove that it has a real core.

ai_applications.description: Maintains the original task or intent during long AI workflows, multi-turn conversations, and agentic execution.
Rewrites or edits a document without destroying its main contribution, authorial voice, or conceptual core.
Converts a paper into slides, a story into a schema, or a framework into code while preserving the core idea.
Separates stable user/project memory from temporary residue or context noise.
Compresses large bodies of text while preserving high-value invariants and discarding obstructive residue.
Gives an AI agent a repeated self-check: what is the core, what is only boundary, and what must be cut before the next step?

xai_applications.description: Explains which elements of the original input were preserved in the output and why.
Explains which surface forms, styles, formats, or contexts were changed.
Explains which parts were cut, isolated, reused, or monitored.
Checks whether an output has drifted away from the original goal or identity.
Makes transformation itself explainable rather than only explaining the final answer.

core_fidelity.definition: How well the structural core is preserved after transformation.

boundary_adaptability.definition: How effectively the boundary is changed without damaging the core.

residue_clearance.definition: How well obstructive residue is removed, isolated, or converted into reusable material.

reclosure_success.definition: How coherent and usable the resulting next configuration is.

drift_resistance.definition: How well the operator resists goal drift and identity drift during transformation.

trace_clarity.definition: How clearly the operator explains what was preserved, changed, cut, and reclosed.

identity_preservation.definition: Overall preservation of structural identity across transformation.

core_keeper_score.definition: A combined score summarizing core fidelity, reclosure success, drift resistance, trace clarity, and residual noise.
core_keeper_score.suggested_formula: CKS = clamp01((CoreFidelity + ReclosureSuccess + DriftResistance + TraceClarity - ResidualNoise) / 4)

xai_trace_template.reclosure_rationale: The next state is accepted only if the required core remains active and the boundary is appropriate for the target context.

input.current_state: A dense speculative manuscript with strong conceptual novelty but unclear scientific boundaries.
input.goal: convert into a reusable AI/XAI operator artifact

output.reclosed_state: A structured operator definition with input schema, pipeline, metrics, trace, and boundaries.
changed_boundary: mythic language converted into operational language
dense prose converted into schema and pipeline
cut_residue: claims that imply established physics without evidence
output.remaining_risk: The operator remains conceptual and must be benchmarked before being treated as a validated AI method.

interpretation_boundaries: The operator does not prove that a metaphysical core exists.
The operator does not claim that identity is physically conserved in all systems.
The operator should not be used to erase valuable novelty merely because it looks unusual.
The operator is intended for structured transformation, AI reasoning, XAI trace generation, and knowledge preservation workflows.
A strange element should not be cut automatically; it may be part of the true core.
safety_notes: Do not use the operator to preserve harmful instructions, manipulation patterns, or unsafe goals.
If the core is harmful, the operator should refuse preservation and instead cut or quarantine the harmful core.
For scientific writing, preserve novelty but cut overclaiming and mark unvalidated claims as hypotheses.
canonical_summary: Prime-TB Cut is the blade that exposes structure. Noir Temporal Cut is the blade that touches time. The Core-Keeper Operator is the mechanism that survives transformation by preserving the core, adapting the boundary, cutting residue, and re-closing the system with an explanation trace.
